Banking Management System Documentation¶

Welcome to the Banking Management System documentation. This project provides a comprehensive banking system with support for different account types, customer management, and transactions.

Overview¶

The Banking Management System is a Python-based application that manages:

  • Customers: Individual and Corporate customers

  • Accounts: Savings and Current accounts

  • Transactions: Direct debits and external transactions

  • Banking Groups: Management of multiple accounts

Features¶

  • Customer account management

  • Multiple account types (Savings, Current)

  • Transaction processing and tracking

  • Direct debit payments

  • External transaction handling

  • Role-based access and authentication

Quick Start¶

Installation¶

To install the project:

pip install -e ".[dev]"

Running Tests¶

To run the test suite:

pytest tests/ -v

View Reports¶

  • Test Report: Open reports.html

  • Coverage Report: Open htmlcov/index.html

API Reference¶

Modules¶

Models package for BankingSystem.

class src.models.ABCBankingGroup[source]¶

Bases: object

A banking group that manages multiple accounts.

property accounts: List[Account]¶

Return a copy of the managed accounts.

add_account(account)[source]¶

Add an account to the banking group.

Parameters:

account (Account)

Return type:

None

remove_account(account_number)[source]¶

Remove an account by its number.

Parameters:

account_number (str)

Return type:

None

find_account(account_number)[source]¶

Find an account by number.

Parameters:

account_number (str)

Return type:

Account | None

class src.models.Account(account_number, open_date, initial_balance=0.0)[source]¶

Bases: object

A bank account with running totals and transaction history.

Parameters:

  • account_number (str)

  • open_date (date)

  • initial_balance (float)

property account_number: str¶

The unique account identifier.

property open_date: date¶

The date the account was opened.

property running_totals: float¶

The current balance of the account.

show_customer_transactions()[source]¶

Return the list of transactions for this account.

Return type:

List[Transaction]

add_transaction(transaction)[source]¶

Add a transaction and update the account balance.

Parameters:

transaction (Transaction)

Return type:

None

class src.models.Corporate(account_number, name, address, contact_number, email, password, company_type)[source]¶

Bases: Customer

A corporate customer with a company type.

Parameters:

  • account_number (str)

  • name (str)

  • address (str)

  • contact_number (str)

  • email (str)

  • password (str)

  • company_type (str)

property company_type: str¶

The legal company type for the corporate customer.

class src.models.Customer(account_number, name, address, contact_number, email, password)[source]¶

Bases: object

A customer with accounts and contact information.

Parameters:

  • account_number (str)

  • name (str)

  • address (str)

  • contact_number (str)

  • email (str)

  • password (str)

property account_number: str¶

The customer account identifier.

property name: str¶

The customer full name.

property address: str¶

The customer address.

property contact_number: str¶

The customer’s contact phone number.

property email: str¶

The customer’s email address.

add_account(account_number)[source]¶

Link an account to this customer.

Parameters:

account_number (str)

Return type:

None

remove_account(account_number)[source]¶

Unlink an account from this customer.

Parameters:

account_number (str)

Return type:

None

classmethod total_number_of_customers()[source]¶

Return the total number of created customers.

Return type:

int

class src.models.CurrentAccount(account_number, open_date, overdraft_limit=0.0, initial_balance=0.0)[source]¶

Bases: Account

A current account that supports overdraft.

Parameters:

  • account_number (str)

  • open_date (date)

  • overdraft_limit (float)

  • initial_balance (float)

property overdraft_limit: float¶

The allowed overdraft limit for the account.

can_withdraw(amount)[source]¶

Return whether the requested withdrawal is allowed.

Parameters:

amount (float)

Return type:

bool

class src.models.DirectDebit(amount, sender, receiver, payment_date, timestamp=None)[source]¶

Bases: Transaction

A transaction scheduled for direct debit on a payment date.

Parameters:

  • amount (float)

  • sender (str)

  • receiver (str)

  • payment_date (date)

  • timestamp (datetime | None)

property payment_date: date¶

The date the direct debit will be processed.

class src.models.ExternalTransaction(amount, sender, receiver, branch_name, branch_address, branch_postcode, branch_code, timestamp=None)[source]¶

Bases: Transaction

A transaction executed through an external branch.

Parameters:

  • amount (float)

  • sender (str)

  • receiver (str)

  • branch_name (str)

  • branch_address (str)

  • branch_postcode (str)

  • branch_code (str)

property branch_name: str¶

The name of the external branch.

property branch_address: str¶

The address of the external branch.

property branch_postcode: str¶

The postcode of the external branch.

property branch_code: str¶

The branch code for the external transaction.

class src.models.Individual(account_number, name, address, contact_number, email, password, surname, gender, date_of_birth)[source]¶

Bases: Customer

A customer who is an individual person.

Parameters:

  • account_number (str)

  • name (str)

  • address (str)

  • contact_number (str)

  • email (str)

  • password (str)

  • surname (str)

  • gender (str)

  • date_of_birth (date)

property surname: str¶

The customer’s surname.

property gender: str¶

The customer’s gender.

property date_of_birth: date¶

The customer’s date of birth.

work_out_age()[source]¶

Calculate the customer’s age from date of birth.

Return type:

int

class src.models.Menu[source]¶

Bases: object

A service layer for managing customers, accounts, and transactions.

property transaction_list: List[Transaction]¶

All recorded transactions.

property customer_account_list: List[Account]¶

Open customer accounts.

property customer_list: List[Customer]¶

Registered customers.

initiate_transaction(transaction)[source]¶

Record a transaction in the system.

Parameters:

transaction (Transaction)

Return type:

None

add_customer(customer)[source]¶

Add a new customer to the system.

Parameters:

customer (Customer)

Return type:

None

delete_customer(account_number)[source]¶

Remove a customer by account number.

Parameters:

account_number (str)

Return type:

None

open_account(account)[source]¶

Open a customer account.

Parameters:

account (Account)

Return type:

None

close_account(account_number)[source]¶

Close an account by account number.

Parameters:

account_number (str)

Return type:

None

edit_customer_details(account_number, *, name=None, address=None, contact_number=None, email=None)[source]¶

Update customer contact information.

Parameters:

  • account_number (str)

  • name (str | None)

  • address (str | None)

  • contact_number (str | None)

  • email (str | None)

Return type:

None

login(account_number, password)[source]¶

Check whether customer credentials are valid.

Parameters:

  • account_number (str)

  • password (str)

Return type:

bool

logout()[source]¶

Placeholder for logout behavior.

Return type:

None

class src.models.SavingsAccount(account_number, open_date, interest_rate=0.0, initial_balance=0.0)[source]¶

Bases: Account

A savings account with an interest rate.

Parameters:

  • account_number (str)

  • open_date (date)

  • interest_rate (float)

  • initial_balance (float)

property interest_rate: float¶

The interest rate applied to the account balance.

calculate_interest()[source]¶

Calculate interest based on the current balance.

Return type:

float

class src.models.Transaction(amount, sender, receiver, timestamp=None)[source]¶

Bases: object

A financial transaction between sender and receiver.

Parameters:

  • amount (float)

  • sender (str)

  • receiver (str)

  • timestamp (datetime | None)

classmethod deposit_money(amount, sender, receiver)[source]¶

Create a deposit transaction.

Parameters:

  • amount (float)

  • sender (str)

  • receiver (str)

Return type:

Transaction

classmethod withdraw_money(amount, sender, receiver)[source]¶

Create a withdrawal transaction.

Parameters:

  • amount (float)

  • sender (str)

  • receiver (str)

Return type:

Transaction
